title: Meiosis and genetic diversity
description: Test your knowledge of meiosis and genetic diversity!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes meiosis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Meiosis begins with a diploid parent cell and ends with four haploid daughter cells.
Meiosis begins with a diploid parent cell and ends with two diploid daughter cells.
Meiosis begins with a haploid parent cell and ends with two haploid daughter cells.
Meiosis begins with a haploid parent cell and ends with four haploid daughter cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes how meiosis I and meiosis II differ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pairs of homologous chromosomes are separated during meiosis I, while sister chromatids are separated during meiosis II.
Cytokinesis occurs following meiosis II but not meiosis I.
Genetic material is exchanged between homologous chromatids during meiosis II but not meiosis I.
Meiosis I produces diploid daughter cells, while meiosis II produces haploid daughter cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
Even though humans are approximately $99.9\%$ identical in their genetic makeup, an incredible amount of variation is seen among individuals of the species. This genetic variation is greatly influenced by the process of meiosis.

**Of the following events that occur during meiosis, which contributes most to genetic diversity in the human population?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Recombination between homologous chromatids during meiosis I
Separation of sister chromatids during meiosis II
Condensation of chromatin during meiosis I
Alignment of sister chromatids at the metaphase plate during meiosis II

assessment_items.question_content:
During meiosis I, pairs of homologous chromosomes align randomly along the metaphase plate before being separated. This process helps generate genetic variation in the offspring of sexual reproduction.

**Which of the following best explains how the random alignment of homologous chromosomes during meiosis helps generate varied offspring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Random alignment increases the number of possible chromosome combinations in the gametes.
Random alignment results in the formation of gametes that contain different numbers of chromosomes.
Random alignment increases the likelihood of crossing over between non-sister chromatids.
Random alignment results in the formation of some diploid and some haploid gametes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gametogenesis, or the formation of gametes, occurs when diploid germ cells undergo meiosis. In fruit flies (*Drosophila melanogaster*), diploid germ cells contain eight chromosomes.  

**Which of the following correctly describes the number of chromosomes in the daughter cells produced during gametogenesis in fruit flies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The daughter cells each contain four chromosomes after meiosis I and four chromosomes after meiosis II.
The daughter cells each contain eight chromosomes after meiosis I and four chromosomes after meiosis II.
The daughter cells each contain eight chromosomes after meiosis I and eight chromosomes after meiosis II.
The daughter cells each contain four chromosomes after meiosis I and two chromosomes after meiosis II.

assessment_items.question_content:
The formation of ova, or female reproductive cells, occurs when diploid germ cells undergo meiosis. In female swamp wallabies (*Wallabia bicolor*), diploid germ cells contain $10$ chromosomes.  

**At the beginning of meiosis I, a female wallaby germ cells contains**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $5$ homologous pairs of chromosomes comprising a total of $20$ chromatids
$5$ homologous pairs of chromosomes comprising a total of $10$ chromatids
$10$ homologous pairs of chromosomes comprising a total of $20$ chromatids
$10$ homologous pairs of chromosomes comprising a total of $10$ chromatids

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes how mitosis and meiosis are similar?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sister chromatids segregate to opposite poles of the cell during both mitosis and meiosis.
Crossing over occurs between homologous chromatids during both mitosis and meiosis.
Both mitosis and meiosis produce diploid daughter cells.
Both mitosis and meiosis produce genetically identical daughter cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes how mitosis and meiosis are different?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mitosis produces genetically identical daughter cells, while meiosis produces genetically unique daughter cells.
Sister chromatids separate during meiosis but not mitosis.
The number of chromosomes is doubled after mitosis, while the number of chromosomes remains the same after meiosis.
DNA replication occurs prior to mitosis but not meiosis.
